title: THE RESCUE — LIFELINE CUT V6 FINAL
episodeObjective: Reach the unstable emergency pod, control the rescue route, expose Hale's old access log, and commit to opening or aborting the pod rescue.

summary: Scenario spine preserved: rescue junction, beacon stabilization, route selection, pod exterior, Hale's old access, access protocol, open/abort branch.
All long physical or technical tasks use characterBusy + returnNodeId.
No progress bars or visible wait timers are used.
Maya returns from each absence with concrete physical and emotional reports.
Every visible choice set has exactly two choices.
Hale/Maya conflict is character-driven and escalates naturally.
Logs are short evidence beats, not the main gameplay.
Open/abort decision creates irreversible Episode 10 flags.

e09_n01.speaker: MAYA
e09_n01.objective: Set the rescue target and immediate conflict.

events.text: I'm at the rescue junction.
The pod beacon is ahead, but it keeps sliding in and out of the relay map like it doesn't want to be found.
Life support is dropping. I can see the pulse in the telemetry.
I heard Noah's voice in the last transmission. Or something that knew exactly how to hurt me.
Nobody touches that hatch until we know what is moving inside it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That's easy for the dead engineer to say.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Stabilizing first.
Good. Technical work. Cables, relays, math. Things that don't pretend to be dead friends.
I'm holding position while Hale filters the drifting pod signal.
I need to keep the relay aligned. If I move, the beacon starts lying again.
Beacon hold is set.
The pod is still moving, but at least now it's lying in a straight line.
Signal is cleaner. Not safe. Cleaner.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I hate that I can hear it better now.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Moving now.
Fast route to visual range. I know you're going to hate this, Hale.
You're rushing.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Someone might be dying.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm clipping to the rail and pushing through before the map shifts again.
I have partial visual on the pod.
It is closer than the map said. That should not be possible.
The beacon dragged across my display while I moved, like the pod was correcting where I thought it was.
So either the map is wrong, or the pod moved toward me.

events.title: RESCUE ROUTE MAP
events.content:
ROUTE A: Short route through exposed radiation corridor. Arrival fast. Crew risk severe. Pod risk moderate.

ROUTE B: Long route through maintenance spine. Arrival delayed. Crew risk moderate. Pod risk severe.
events.caption: Recovered rescue route analysis. Both routes carry consequences.
events.message: [RESCUE ROUTE MAP ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Two paths. Neither one is good.
Route A gets me there fast, but I take the radiation hit.
Route B keeps me safer, but the pod loses time.
Pick the sin you can live with, Operator.
Fast and dangerous. Very ECHO.
Radiation corridor ahead. The warning lights are dead, which feels rude.
Do not stop in the exposed section.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I was not planning a picnic, Ethan.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Going quiet. I need to run this clean.
I cleared the radiation corridor.
My suit alarm is being very rude about my life choices.
The corridor hummed through my teeth. I didn't know that was a thing a place could do.
But I'm closer. If my hair falls out, I'm blaming you. Emotionally. Not legally.
Taking the maintenance spine.
Safer for me. Worse for the clock.
This is the sensible path.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Sensible is a nice word for slow when someone is suffocating.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I need to crawl most of this. Don't expect charm from me when I get back.
I'm at the pod exterior.
I found older boot marks in the dust. Someone used this route before us.
One set stops halfway and turns back. That's comforting in exactly no way.
I got here safer. The pod lost time. Both facts are sitting in my chest.
I'm at the pod.
The hull is frosted from the inside.
Noah used to draw faces in condensation on viewport glass. Terrible faces. Like potatoes with opinions.
Sorry. Memory leak. Human version.

events.title: POD EXTERIOR SCAN
events.content:
Hull Temperature: below operational range
Seal Pressure: unstable
Interior Motion: intermittent
Audio Contamination: rising
Manual Override: possible
Warning: signal distortion resembles recursive voice fold
events.caption: Exterior scan of the pod. The pod is physically reachable but unstable.
events.message: [POD EXTERIOR SCAN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choices.text: SECURE THE POD BEFORE TOUCHING THE HATCH.

events.text: Securing the pod first.
Anchoring line is out. If it moves, I move with it instead of under it.
That is somehow both reassuring and horrifying.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Anchor holds.
The pod stopped fighting the drift.
I wish that sentence felt less alive.
The line vibrates every few seconds, like something inside is answering the beacon.
Going to the hatch.
The pod is still drifting. I see it.
Maya, back off. The pod is still moving.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I need one hand on the rail and one on the hatch frame.
The pod shifted.
I hit the rail. I'm fine.
That was a lie. My shoulder disagrees, but it's still attached.
The hatch is reachable, but the whole frame is carrying motion through it.
I need to tell you something before you touch the override.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I have seen this pod before.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Before Alpha. It was empty.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No body. No suit. No blood. Just frost on the inside of the glass.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You opened it?
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Manual inspection.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That is such an Ethan answer.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: OLD POD ACCESS LOG
events.content:
Access Event: Emergency Pod Manual Inspection
Timestamp: Before Incident Alpha
Operator: Ethan Hale
Pod State: empty
Interior Residue: frost / no biological matter detected
Seal Reclosure: completed manually
Current Conflict: active motion and biometrics now detected inside same pod
events.caption: Recovered old access log. Hale previously opened the pod before Alpha.
events.message: [OLD POD ACCESS LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: The log backs him up.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I hate when a bad answer becomes a documented bad answer.
Empty before Alpha. Moving now.
So either something got in...
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Or something was always there and the system learned not to see it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Staying on the hatch.
I know Hale wants records. I know records matter.
But the hatch is real and the clock is real and whatever is breathing in there is real enough for the pod to keep it alive.

event.text: Reality is exactly what this station edits best.
event.speaker: HALE

event.text: Then keep talking while I work.
event.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I am exposing the manual override now.
Override handle is exposed.
It looks old enough to hate me personally.
The hatch clicked twice while I was working. Not metal settling. A deliberate sound.

events.title: POD ACCESS RISK REPORT
events.content:
Interior Motion: detected
Seal Pressure: unstable
Manual Override: required
Audio Channel Contamination: rising
External Voice Traffic: unauthorized
Recommendation: choose access protocol before opening
events.caption: Recovered access risk report. The pod can be opened, but the audio channel is contaminated.
events.message: [POD ACCESS RISK REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Manual override is ready.
The pod keeps making little pressure clicks. Like fingernails against glass.
Operator. Decide how I open it.
Careful means slower. Fast means I may not control what comes out.
Venting first.
That buys control.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: And costs time.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm bleeding pressure from the pod seal. I need to keep the valve steady.
Pressure is stable.
The voice got weaker during venting.
I don't know if that is good or monstrous.
The hatch is safer now. The thing inside sounds less human.
Forcing it.
Maya, no—
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Do not thank me yet.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm bypassing pressure sequencing and pulling the release.
Seal released hard.
Something hit the inside of the hatch.
I heard it through my gloves.
The pod wants open now. I can feel it fighting the latch.
Last chance.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If you open it, you commit to whatever is inside.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If you abort, you may leave a person to die.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I hate both options.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Good. That means you understand them.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Opening it.
I have internal air mixing.
Keep your face away from the seam.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: There is someone inside.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm releasing the final hatch lock.
The hatch is open enough to see a glove.
NV on the wrist.
If this is a trap, it knows exactly which dead man to wear.
He's breathing. Or the pod is making him look like he is.
Aborting.
I hate you a little right now.
Not forever. Maybe not even fairly. But right now.

event.text: Maya, step away from the hatch.
event.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm trying.
I need to pull away before the beacon talks me back into this.
I'm clear.
The beacon is still following us.
That feels like an answer we didn't ask for.
I can still hear it clicking through the line. One tap. Two taps. Like Noah used to do.

directorCut.sourceTitle: THE RESCUE — DIRECTOR'S CUT V5 UPGRADED
directorCut.coreObjective: Guide Maya through the rescue route and commit to opening or aborting the emergency pod.
majorChanges: All progressTask sections replaced with characterBusy events and return nodes.
Every long physical operation now pauses chat and resumes with a report.
Maya's route choices have concrete physical consequences.
Hale's old pod access log remains a major reveal.
Open/abort remains the irreversible branch into Episode 10.
Noah/pod ambiguity is strengthened without adding a matching puzzle.
All visible choices are exactly two options.
